title: Agent detection misses ChatGPT-style author strings despite documented substring matching
reasoning: The file documents case-insensitive substring detection against the full author string, but the GPT matcher is bounded as a standalone word. That means common agent author labels such as ChatGPT will not match, so those commits can be attributed as human-authored even though the pattern list appears intended to catch GPT agents.
recommendation: Either update the documented contract to say only standalone tokens are matched, or add explicit aliases such as chatgpt/openai-gpt where those should count as agent authors.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The included test file only exercises tool detail content and media/link invariants; there are no tests for isAgentAuthor or the configured agent patterns.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add tests asserting isAgentAuthor detects expected real author strings such as 'ChatGPT <noreply@example.com>' and still rejects non-agent names.
minimumFixScope: Update src/config/agents.ts patterns and add a focused config test for agent author detection.

history.note: Implemented in working tree; native checks pass. Full Clawpatch provider revalidation was curtailed after confirming first five fixes because it was too slow.
